comment_text:
For those who dont get the show:

I didnt get it too at first but that's because all the millionaires so far were weak players imo (im up to ep 4). 

If for example Phil were a stronger player he could lie about what his reward was and say "all votes against me are not counted" when in fact its something else, thats a really powerful tool to be able to convince a few people to SIDE WITH YOU.  
And if you're a non millionaire it would be greatly in your advantage if the millionaire does side with you.

The true nature of this game is to find the millionaire NOT to get them out but to get the them to side with you and then backstab them right AT THE END. 

Like i said im up to ep 4 but Lauren and Phil were weak players tbh

video_title: Million Dollar Secret | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I have a reality show idea that Netflix should totally make!
It’s called Incognito — still a survival show of deception, manipulation, and lies but with a twist.
15 strangers live together in a house or villa, but the catch is they must stay strangers. They can only use their real first names and are forbidden to reveal anything about their job, net income, one big fact about their profession, and maybe even things like their work location or industry.
At the start, a board will show a list of all professions in the house (but not who has which one).
The players have to socialize, investigate, and fish out clues about each other through conversations.
Each day, they’ll have challenges to win a clue about someone’s identity, income, or career.
At night, there’s an elimination: to kick someone out, you must correctly guess their profession. If you guess wrong, you’re at risk too!
It keeps going until only one player remains and wins the prize money.
INCREDIBLE potential for drama, strategy, and mind games. Netflix, make this happen!!
Plus the contestants are random in all ages and their careers like someone is a successful business owner or someone is like a police officer, someone is like an HR or a film director etc.

comment_text: If it wasn't for my wife who likes these types of shows, I would have been happy to never have watched it. The people Netflix picks for these shows have never proved me wrong that they are idiots. Case in point, or should I say points... Lauren's dumb ass tells everyone she was the millionaire making her an outcast. Five people voted for Lauren, knowing she wasn't the millionaire only because she lied about being the millionaire. As if she was supposed to tell people she was the millionaire.  Duh! Of course, if they were the millionaire, they would have lied also. Then Phil, when given the opportunity to take the heat off himself after the first tie vote, voted with his feelings knowing his vote as the millionaire would break the tie. As if he and Sydney could win the million dollars together. And even after the vote made him blatantly look like the millionaire, it took them two days to even consider Phil. Honestly, I'm just gonna move my PS5 to the living room so my wife can watch this dumb show by herself.

video_title: Million Dollar Secret | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
SPOILER ALERT 🚨

Just finished the show and OMG it was amazing! I have so many thoughts I need to get off my chest.

First of all, I was REALLY hoping that Kyle, Sam, or Corey would win the million dollars — they honestly earned it in my opinion. I’m actually so glad Sydnee didn’t win because her attitude was awful, and the fact that she kills animals? No thanks.

Sam had a real shot at winning, but she lost it when her emotions got in the way — especially when it came to someone she cared about, like Cara. That just shows how soft-hearted Sam actually is, which kind of made her lose her edge as a cop.

As for Jamie, I think she was mostly honest throughout the show. But the way she naturally acted came off as weird to the others, so instead of suspecting the real snake (Cara), they pointed fingers at Jamie.

And speaking of Cara — wow. She played it SO well. This show really teaches you that you can’t trust people like her. And unfortunately, both the guys and Sam made the mistake of trusting her, and she ended up walking away with the million.

Corey, or Kyle instead.
❤❤❤❤❤


P.S Also so glad that Lauren didn’t win either — she was incredibly fake and sneaky the whole time. Seriously, watch out for people like that in real life too!
